annotations.label: Partition of a set (informal)

anchor.snippet: a partition of a set is a grouping of its elements into non-empty subsets

annotations.note: `Setoid.IsPartition c` formalizes a collection of sets with no empty set and each element in a unique set.

annotations.label: Equivalence relations correspond to partitions

anchor.snippet: Every equivalence relation on a set defines a partition of this set, and every partition defines an equivalence relation.

annotations.note: `Setoid.Partition.orderIso` provides an order-preserving bijection between `Setoid α` and `Partitions α`.

annotations.label: Setoid

anchor.snippet: A set equipped with an equivalence relation or a partition is sometimes called a setoid

annotations.note: `Setoid` is a core Lean structure bundling an equivalence relation, used throughout Mathlib.

annotations.label: Partition of a set X

anchor.snippet: A partition of a set X is a set of non-empty subsets of X such that every element x in X is in exactly one of these subsets

annotations.note: `Setoid.IsPartition c := ∅ ∉ c ∧ ∀ a, ∃! b ∈ c, a ∈ b` matches the definition verbatim.

annotations.label: Equivalent conditions for a partition

anchor.snippet: Equivalently, a family of sets P is a partition of X if and only if all of the following conditions hold

annotations.note: `IsPartition.pairwiseDisjoint`, `IsPartition.sUnion_eq_univ`, and `Set.PairwiseDisjoint.isPartition_of_exists_of_ne_empty` together give the equivalence.

annotations.label: Blocks/parts/cells of a partition

anchor.snippet: are called the blocks , parts , or cells , of the partition

annotations.note: `Finpartition.parts` and `Partition.parts` use the terminology, but only as a field accessor without a separate `blocks`/`cells` synonym.

annotations.label: Partition identified with equivalence relation

anchor.snippet: Every partition

annotations.note: `Setoid.Partition.orderIso` establishes the canonical identification used in this passage.

annotations.label: Partition of the empty set

anchor.snippet: has exactly one partition

annotations.note: No statement uniquely characterizing the (empty) partition of the empty set as a `Setoid.IsPartition` is present.

annotations.label: Trivial partition

anchor.snippet: P = { X } is a partition of X , called the trivial partition

annotations.note: `Finpartition.indiscrete` is the indiscrete/trivial partition consisting of a single part.

annotations.label: Partition by subset and complement

anchor.snippet: the set A together with its complement form a partition of U

annotations.note: No specific lemma asserting `{A, Aᶜ}` is a `Setoid.IsPartition` was found.

annotations.label: Five partitions of {1,2,3}

anchor.snippet: The set {1, 2, 3} has these five partitions

annotations.note: No enumeration of the partitions of a specific 3-element set is formalized.

annotations.label: Non-partitions of {1,2,3}

anchor.snippet: The following are not partitions of {1, 2, 3}

annotations.note: No counterexamples for `IsPartition` on a specific 3-element set are formalized.

annotations.label: Equivalence classes form a partition

anchor.snippet: For any equivalence relation on a set X , the set of its equivalence classes is a partition of X .

annotations.note: `Setoid.isPartition_classes : IsPartition r.classes` is exactly this statement.

annotations.label: Axiom of choice and transversal

anchor.snippet: The axiom of choice guarantees for any partition of a set X the existence of a subset of X containing exactly one element from each part of the partition.

annotations.note: `IndexedPartition.out` and `Quotient.out` give representatives for indexed partitions/setoids, but no direct `Setoid.IsPartition` transversal lemma was found.

annotations.label: Refinement of a partition

anchor.snippet: A partition α of a set X is a refinement of a partition ρ of X

annotations.note: `Finpartition`'s `LE` instance defines refinement (each part of `P` is `≤` some part of `Q`); `Partition` and `Partitions` carry analogous orders.

annotations.label: Partition lattice

anchor.snippet: This "finer-than" relation on the set of partitions of X is a partial order

annotations.note: `Setoid.Partition.completeLattice` gives a `CompleteLattice (Partitions α)`; `Finpartition.instPartialOrder` gives the partial order on `Finpartition`.

annotations.label: Meet and join of partitions

anchor.snippet: The meet and join of partitions α and ρ are defined as follows.

annotations.note: `Setoid.completeLattice` provides `⊓`/`⊔` on equivalence relations, transported to `Partitions α` via `Setoid.Partition.completeLattice`.

annotations.label: Lattice of partitions and graphic matroid

anchor.snippet: Based on the equivalence between geometric lattices and matroids , this lattice of partitions of a finite set corresponds to a matroid

annotations.note: No formalization of geometric lattices or the graphic matroid via the partition lattice was found.

annotations.label: Card deck refinement

anchor.snippet: If D is the set of cards in a standard 52-card deck

annotations.note: This concrete card-deck example is not formalized.

annotations.label: Noncrossing partition

anchor.snippet: is noncrossing if it has the following property

annotations.note: A grep for `Noncrossing`/`noncrossing` in `Mathlib/` returned no matches.

annotations.label: Noncrossing partition lattice is not a sublattice

anchor.snippet: The lattice of noncrossing partitions of a finite set forms a subset of the lattice of all partitions, but not a sublattice

annotations.note: Noncrossing partitions are absent from Mathlib, so this statement is unformalized.

annotations.label: Bell number counts partitions

anchor.snippet: The total number of partitions of an n -element set is the Bell number

annotations.note: `Nat.bell` is defined and its recurrence proved, but the file's TODO notes that it is not yet proved to count partitions.

annotations.label: Stirling number of the second kind

anchor.snippet: The number of partitions of an n -element set into exactly k (non-empty) parts is the Stirling number of the second kind

annotations.note: `Nat.stirlingSecond` is defined via its recurrence; no theorem equating it to a count of `Setoid.IsPartition`s was found.

annotations.label: Catalan number counts noncrossing partitions

anchor.snippet: The number of noncrossing partitions of an n -element set is the Catalan number

annotations.note: `Nat.catalan` exists but with no link to (unformalized) noncrossing partitions.
